title: Trying 3 American School Lunches
body: The best school lunch I ever had was in elementary school in Puerto Rico (2002-2008). No microwaved food, it was all cooked from scratch by the cafeteria ladies. Breakfast: Freshly made oatmeal, cream of wheat, fresh fruits, etc.. Lunch: Anything from Rice, beans, and chicken to chinese rice. Fried chicken, beef, vegetables, etc.. On "special days" we would get hot dogs, or rarely pizza. Definitely miss those times, even more when I moved out of Puerto Rico in 2011.

title: Trying 3 American School Lunches

title: Trying 3 American School Lunches
body: I'm from Germany and my school lunch from 1984 to 1990 was gray bread with cold cuts or cheese, with apple or carrot pieces on the side. So very similar to your grandfathers, but with better bread. We drank milk or chocolate milk we bought in school. I got a weekly allowance later on and would buy stuff on the way to school in a bakery.

title: Trying 3 American School Lunches
body: My school lunch is filling and probably the most nostalgic thing I ever had. It's was my lunch when I was Elementary and it is composed of 3 cups of rice compacted in my lunchbox, in a different container have a variety of lunch food pairing with rice like bitter gourd omelette, dragon beard noodle soup, chicken adobo, pork adobo, pumpkin and noodle stew and even mung bean with pork stew and vermicelli noodles. Different dishes everyday, and if I'm lucky I also get a fried egg. It was prepared by my beloved mom and I still thank her for it because her food is delicious 😋 I wish I could go back to that time and experience it once more
author: @Ian_Knife

title: Oxford student reacts to India’s JEE Advanced exam paper *really hard* #shorts #viral #jeeadvanced
body: I remember eating at this Indian restaurant where the waiters would hold iPads when they were taking orders. Initially, I thought it was just for the convenience of them taking the order and inputting the price immediately but I was wrong. They memorized the orders, including a ton of customization on the dishes, and were able to calculate the total price the customers have to pay. And the iPad, well, it was just for them to show the customers and prove their mental calculation is correct. From that day on, Indian people have more of my respect than they already did

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: Europe is so diverse that we cannot speak of the European way of life as a whole, but of each individual, by country, by region and, sometimes, by town or village.
author: @WolfearOfficial

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: You kind of prove the American stereotype by speaking of Europe as a singular entity. Life in France can be drastically different from life in Poland or Czech, for example. This doesn't mean only different languages, but also different costs of living, different mentality, different cultures and references, often different ways of life.
author: @capricorn3511

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: culture not only means music cinema and media, it often means (for us europeans) general knowledge, history and geography, books, art, politics, social awareness of the world, and all those things. it's this kind of culture were we think usa lacks
author: @lorenzopierangeli6349

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: I lived in LA for about 10 years now. I was born and raised in a small town in Italy. The change has been drastic for me. It’s very hard to draw a line on what’s better and worse. I’m a European at heart and I prefer it. However, the US gave me all the chances I could ask for, and I can’t be grateful enough. The US is a society free for the most part from things such as nepotism, and is instead merit based. In Italy where I’m from it’s not about who you are , but better who you know and what can you leverage for yourself with you connections. All the world is like that but in Italy matters more than anything. I will definitely be back in Europe at some point , but the US gave me so much and nobody cared about my background, they only cared about what I could bring to the table. In Europe even if I were to work that hard, I’m not sure I would have accomplished the same things
author: @AxiA

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: As a Swede who has visited most countries in Europe, as well as many countries outside of Europe (including for instance India and USA), and who is now living in Brazil since about 5 years, I can say that as long as you have money – it doesn't really matter where you live. At that point the weather probably becomes the most important point.
author: @marcusbrsp

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: I grew up in the U.S. and I completely identify with what you said about the speed of life. In the U.S. I’m considered an extremely “slow” eater to the point that my friends will ditch me at a restaurant as I eat my meal because they finished half an hour before me. But when I went to Spain, I went out to eat and received comments about how quickly I ate as I finished my meal way before anyone else. People in Europe simply cherish the time they have and connect with the food as opposed to wolfing it down and getting back to work ASAP in the U.S.
author: @JohnGaroPiano

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body:
I was raised in the US and moved to Spain when i was 27. Six years later when i came back to visit the homeland i noticed my friends would kind of brag to each other about how many hours they worked the last week. I was just quietly thinking 'that sounds like a horrible week' no time for anything at all. Life is short and you are only getting older. Nobody on their death bead ever said 'you know, i wish i had put in more 60+ hour weeks' 😂
I'm sitting there way more relaxed than anybody else, with way more stories to share and experiences lived just over those few years. All because of a cultural difference where Europeans feel that it's almost immoral and a dis-service to your valuable human life to waste it away by clocking hours whether it's for your own business, or for another person's business. I work to live vs I live to work is a real big difference between the USA and Europe.
author: @Pinionaire

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: No hate, just a note: USA isn't a continent, it's a country, europe is a continent and the EU is an alliance
author: @EvilBorb

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: When he said Europeans feel like eating that fast is almost morally wrong, I really felt it. Having lunch with other people or even by yourself has such a deep meaning for most of us, even if it's just for 20 or 30 minutes cause one day you just don't have time. I'm glad you mentioned it. Greetings from Spain
author: @jakesmith8710

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: Just to also mention that European countries can be wildly different to each other. Life in Italy or Spain is very, very different to life in the UK or Ireland. Each have their pros and cons, you just have to know what vibe you're looking for.
author: @BlackCoffeeee

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: What shocked me as a belgian visiting NYC is how there were almost no terraces where you could sit down and enjoy a beer. The absence of that is unthinkable in most EU countries. There's bars with terraces everywhere.
author: @nickverschoore4351

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: As Argentinian living in the US, i almost  suffer depression with suburban life here.  No kids playing on the streets or people walking around or something very typical in my country is simply sitting on the sidewalk with a couple chairs in the neighborhood or in the balcony / patio. I never see anyone playing on their yards despite they are huge, everything is souless and empty. I moved to a big city because it's the closest thing i could get- people still drive a lot here and are way lazier, but there's more foot traffic and people interacting with each other on the streets.
author: @valencappe1352

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: As a 23 i moved to Poland , what a culture shock it was. Safe , clean and the food my man the food
author: @johnfu9863

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: As an American living in the midwest, I find myself craving the kind of closeness and cultural diversity that you talk about with Europe... here, everyone has big houses and big yards but no one really comes over to visit... You have to drive 10 minutes to get to a store, everyone feels so isolated and it's honestly so lonely here...
author: @mikeware5567

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: I was born and raised in the United States and was fortunate to have lived in Germany for several years, many years ago.  One thing that I have never forgotten was the time when several of us gathered at a Gasthaus for a few beers, and while there, was invited to join in the conversations that involved others from several different countries.  What amazed me was the mutual respect shown by everyone, to everyone, regardless of their gender, nationality, or beliefs. This 'mutual acceptance' is becoming more and more difficult to find within the US these days.
author: @WarHawk-

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: As a German, I feel quite isolated in New Zealand. Just to get to the supermarket, it's a ten-minute drive. The sidewalks are empty, as everyone drives from one place to another. Cafes and shops are so far apart and scattered that it's not appealing to walk around the streets. It's very much like America here.
author: @MinimalWave1982

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: As a Dutch citizen, I genuinely think both the USA and Europe have very different but very interesting cultures with pros/cons depending on your preference of course, but living in the USA now for my exchange makes me realize how much I appreciate the relaxed life in Europe where (from eating food to working) everyone values and respects their time off. The same thing goes for food whereas in Europe we care about the quality and certain artificial ingredients are banned because they're unhealthy while in the USA they're still used simply to make the food look more appealing... I love the USA for so many reasons, but I appreciate Europe even more because of how seriously our health and time off is taken here
author: @Dani-pf2ti

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: Im from the US, but I went to pursue my masters degree in England and I loved it. I got to travel to Amsterdam, France, Spain and Italy. I lived in Spain for 6 months. Life in Europe is so stress free and calm, I love it there.
author: @maridee4476

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: Such an insightful breakdown of the pros and cons! It’s fascinating how 'quality of life' can mean different things on each side of the Atlantic. This video really got me reflecting on what 'better' actually means. Thanks for tackling this big question so honestly!
author: @Introstellars

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: I've lived in Europe for five years and recently moved to the US and I can totally vouch for the reverse cultural effect. I do find it easier to communicate in English here but I really miss the ease of making friends and having a general good vibe when going around any place in Europe. Like G Gold said below, "no one hangs around like the europeans". I would any day love to go back there.
author: @azterixz1234

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: After moving to France (from Texas) 30 years ago, I can honestly say that every day is a thrill, enriching, rewarding, intellectually stimulating. Even being lonely is more interesting in Europe, LOL. Every time I go back to TX for a visit, I'm shocked to see such an emphasis on sports, on religion, and TV. There're usually at least 2 or more TVs in every damn restaurant. Not the case here. Vive la France !
author: @franchic9565

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: I guess as a Frenchman, who lived in Canada, in England and in the US that what we call culture, in France in particular, is this intangible thing that has no commercial value. More exactly, something  on which you cannot put a price. The most iconic moment in French literature that encompasses this idea is the madeleine de Proust. This feeling which comes from a scent, a sight or a taste that gives you a sense of identity. My grand-parents were of Italian origin, they came to France when they were eight, however their cuisine was infused with recipes from their childhood: pasta, gnocchis, capeleti, tortellli that they would prepare the morning before 6. We would take our breakfast with this fresh pasta drying on the table, and we knew that we would have a wonderful meal later on. Every time I go to Italy, this is what I have in my most deepest memory, the remembrance of these meals when I was so lucky to eat fresh Italian food. The real problem that Europeans have with American culture is not that Americans have no culture, but that any culture is bastardized by corporations. That you cannot escape prejudices or stereotypes because they sell. And as a Frenchman, some of these stereotypes were pretty cringy. I think that the first victims of this omnipresence of money and greed from these corporations are the Americans themselves.
author: @lesfreresdelaquote1176

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body:
Nathaniel, I really love your vids, the way you are as a man and the way you think and see things!!!
For me it is hard to compare the US with Europe!
It doesn't matter in the US, if you are in the West, Mid or East, they all speak English and only the accent maybe is different. But in Europe going from the West to the East, even from the North to the South there is not the "one European" or only one language everybody can talk or understand with only different accents like in the US.
Every single country in Europe has their own language and even their own behaviors/identities.
Me, as a German/French guy I am having typical behaviors of the Germans and French you can't just compare. The Americans from the West Coast and East Coast are different, yes, but still they are "Americans". Yes, we are a united Europe, but we don't really see us as a "European" e.g. if we are getting asked on vacation where we are fom. I see myself as a German and if you would ask my cousin in Paris, who is also a German/French, he sees himself as a French. 😀
And all these different cultures, languages, mindsets, behaviors and so on in whole Europe makes Europe so amazing and such a wonderful continent, but definetly not comparable to the US.
author: @k.s.8064

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: My wife, our 10 year old and I moved to Switzerland about 6 months ago for a work opportunity.  We live in a small village about 40 min north of Geneva along the coastline.  Many of your observations ring true to what I have observed since moving here.  A comment I made to my wife last week when we were in Italy for a long weekend was "no one hangs out like a European".  That wasn't a derogatory comment.  I said it with a sense of appreciation.  I have really enjoyed the 1.5 - 2 hour lunches (3+ hour dinners) with our new friends.  Just getting together for a coffee and conversations.  The appreciation for nature and just being outside and experiencing fresh air, wind, sun etc, is fantastic as well.  The slowed pace of life has been really refreshing.  As has the overall sense of "manners".  In the US the idea of good manners has seemed to disappear.  I'm thinking that that will be one of the biggest "reverse culture shocks" that I will experience when we move back home.  As will the quality of food.  I feel so much better here and we are eating so well.  With a major reduction in sugar. Anyway, it's been great so far and I already know that I'll miss most of it when we move back.
author: @ggold3357

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: When I moved to the UK, the thing that most surprised me was how I instantly recieved 30 days of vacation when I started my first job.
author: @vkotis

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: One of the worst culture shocks for me when I visited friends in the US was how socially unacceptable it apparently is to take your time at a restaurant and talk for a while longer even after you finish eating. I understand having to leave when you're done if the restaurant's full and there are people waiting to get a table, but if there are still plenty of free tables, why should i be in a rush to get up? Me and my friends here in Europe always sit around for a little bit longer and talk after finishing our food, and if we're taking longer than 30 minutes we'll simply order another round of drinks. Meanwhile my friends in the US immediately pay and leave as soon as they're done eating, and it's so weird to me especially considering that the US doesn't offer many free public spaces to spend time in and socialize, meaning it's not like you can just leave the restaurant and find a nice park within a few minutes of walking where you could continue the conversation (in many places).
author: @ifailedtheturingtest7866

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: I love your videos how you highlight drivers behind differences. Often it’s a matter of perspective. Neither is good or bad. It’s just different from the other.
author: @ronaldhagara4088

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: I am from Russia, moved to Florida in 2016. The more I live here, the more I feel like I will eventually get back to Europe, just not Russia. Europe feels more relaxed overall, constant work culture and high pressure feel overwhelming here in the US. Always thought that America is for those who want to have a business and work 100 hours a week to earn millions, while Europe is for good work-life balance without catching stars from the sky, yet it is still can be subjective
author: @DanielTheAmericanOne

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Europe? (An Honest Review)
body: When I was 11 I began learning French at school and as soon as I heard it spoken I said that I would one day live in France. I won an art competition at 14 and said then that one day I would live in France again. I lived in a small village in Scotland, no where ! When I was 17 I attended university and at 18 I took my first trip to Paris and immediately felt at home, the lifestyle, the sitting and observing, the weather, the fashion. At 21 after getting my degree I moved to Paris and never looked back. I did move for three years to Brazil then China but came back as soon as possible to France and I now live in the South West of France, very different from cities like Paris but equally wonderful. I go to the famers market in my village, the baker for fresh bread and lovely cakes and I walk the dogs in the botanical park of the local 16 th century chateaux then wind around the river and back home for lunch. I wouldn't change a thing.
author: @TheFiown

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body:
I understand some viewers expected a definitive conclusion given the video’s title, and that’s a fair point. If you came to this video expecting a clear verdict, I can see how the lack of one might feel frustrating. I’ll keep that in mind for future videos.

This video was never meant to hand out a neat, one-size-fits-all answer to a nuanced and deeply personal question.

If I had said, ‘Canada is better’ or ‘the USA is better,’ I’d be oversimplifying something that depends entirely on who you are, what you value, and what your life circumstances look like. That’s why I laid out all the factors—income, cost of living, healthcare, career opportunities, social benefits, and livability—and gave you the tools to weigh them against your own priorities.

Let’s be real—if I claimed that one country is definitively ‘better,’ a lot of people would have (rightfully) called me out for ignoring the nuanced and subjective nature of the topic.

Instead, I made the conscious choice to present an even-handed, data-driven analysis while also sharing my own experiences and the experiences of my guests. My goal was to help you reflect on what’s “better” for you based on what you’re optimizing for.

As for my honest take, I did share it. I said Canada feels safer and offers a better social safety net, while the USA provides unparalleled opportunities for career advancement, which align with my goals right now. That is my honest answer, and that’s why I’m here in the US.

At the end of the day, my goal is to encourage critical thinking, deep conversation, and reflection, not to hand out easy answers. I respect that you might feel differently.

Thanks for watching, and I appreciate everyone who engages thoughtfully in this conversation.
author: @AmeerCorro

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body:
Loved this video. Thank you. Can’t wait to watch the follow up tomorrow.

For my Philly folks wondering about that shot in Philly. That’s 3rd and Roosevelt blvd.
author: @Ashhhten

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: My husband (American) and I (Canadian) doubled our income when we relocated back to Canada. We are now retired but were both teachers. A comment from a former US principal was that I was bringing home the same as him. Anyone who knows the US education system knows that administrators bring home MUCH more than teachers. So basically, I doubled my income with universal healthcare. Also, the respect given teachers was SO much better in Canada, although even that has room for improvement. While in the US we regularly heard that teachers were underworked and overpaid.
author: @JanieFries-z9e

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: As someone who lived in Vancouver Canada 🇨🇦 for a year and moving back to Chicago Illinois 🇺🇸 I would say America 🇺🇸 is better regarding the financially quality of life and the positive economic opportunities that are present there. Next topic: Social quality , id say the Canada 🇨🇦 there is a culture of respect in Vancouver at least. I’ve never meet any serious gangs like you will see in Chicago and New York so it’s safer in Vancouver except in east Hastings street because there are a lot of druggies. I’ve been to east Hastings and many of the druggies have been very polite and respectful. That doesn’t mean they’re all like that. But it’s not like Chicago where gangs and homeless people come up and harass you. I’ve experienced that many times and would recommend to go to Chicago with a group of people unless you’re in Michigan avenue
author: @abij4247

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: @20:30 - Canadian healthcare isn't free. Canadians pay for their healthcare with the taxes that they pay. In some provinces they pay a bit extra each year (kind of like a co-pay) for care while in other provinces, like Ontario, they don't. A friend of mine from Ontario had an emergency appendectomy a few years ago. She went to the emergency department and was admitted a couple of hours later due to the severity of her situation. She had the operation, was in hospital for two nights, was sent home with meds, and her bill was $0.00. My cousin had the same operation in the US and his bill was $65K. Big difference.
author: @suvariboy

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body:

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)

author: @thebroadlife

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: I've spent most of my life living near Vancouver. I lived in Berlin Germany for a few years for work and the lifestyle and cost of living was FAR better. I was making about $4000/month CDN. I had my own nice apartment right downtown, grocery stores were less than a minute walk away, I could afford to eat out at restaurants every day for lunch, I lived very comfortably and I was putting lots of money into savings as well. Then I moved back to the Vancouver area in Canada, I now make around $6000/month CDN, which after tax is actually closer to $3000. I could not afford to live on my own, had to live with my mom in a small townhouse, and after all bills and necessities were covered, I had around $300 to cover groceries, which $300 really doesnt get much. No money yo put away. So my mom and I decided to sell our small townhouse and moved to the middle of nowhere in Northern Alberta on nearly 200 acres. Now I can afford bills, groceries, and have money left over, and I have privacy and lots of land. My mom and I have our own houses on the acreage. I love it, never going back to the city life.
author: @Xiox321

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: This was super interesting!! I dont recall you mentioning child benefits in Canada. My friend gets around $600/month for each kid, and she has 5 so that's a significant release of burden, especially since she is a single parent with a job and going to school all at once!
author: @freshnelly

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: You are looking at this from a GenZ viewpoint. As GenX that has lived in both countries, I would much rather live in a rural area of the US. My largest factor of decision would be that the government is just too large in Canada and using money printing has taken half our savings away in under 5 years, leaving us having to rely on a completely broken system in Canada. My wife recently broke her back and shoulder and the hospital literally kicked her out within 18 hours saying there was no rooms to heal in. She was on morphine and throwing up as I drove her to our offgrid cabin with outhouse when she could barely move.
author: @JohnAdamsCan

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: Wow! I usually do not comment on these videos, but, this one I will. Well done! I am Canadian so I am a little bias but I think you did an excellent job in explaining the differences between the two countries. I will still choose Canada any day to live. Especially now! with Donald in charge. But, I think where you should look at in your next video is the education system for children in Canada compared to the US. I think hands down that we have a better education system. I have actually taught in the states and Canada. In my opinion we have them beat in that system. Keep up the excellent reporting and maybe you should change your career and become a documentarian.

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: What an amazing video! I appreciate how you've compared every important aspect to consider when living somewhere. I've visited Canada once about a decade ago (Toronto and Montreal), and would like to visit again. I can even see these differences between U.S. states. I was born in New Mexico (quite a poor state, but plenty of job opportunities in the arts), and moved to Wisconsin (fewer opportunities in the arts, but a wealthier state). Both states have a similar (low) cost of living, however.

title: Is Life Better in the USA or Canada? (An Honest Review)
body: I appreciate the meassured and detailed analysis. Your values change with what life stages you are in. The tradeoff's and the big sacrifise of moving means people should do deep analysis. Living like a nomad is fine alone but with kids it impacts developmental stages. They struggle forming deep relations and long term relationships.The environment you live in informs your life and at a point satisfaction with your station in life and what and where you can do the best and the most with what you have is most pragmatic. Few people can weather no income or low income at later stages of life with dependents. Changing vocation is really challenging and stressful.  Moving cities is easier.

body: Missing: The oh so funny "Don't walk on this somewhat decorative piece of sidewalk/threshold ."If a student does they will not graduate. If a prospective student does they will not get accepted. And, wait for it... If a parent does they will not get financial aid for their child. (polite laughter) . Also missing the famous story about how a student once called public safety for a ride late at night back to their dorm, and would you believe it, they actually got the ride. This was not the same student who called from the blue light safety phone because they saw a deer. True story!
author: @MarkNotWithAC

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: HPU has taken over the area, buying up so many businesses and homes. The growth throughout my 36 years been insane.
author: @hheidii

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: Great vid. Grew up on campus, my mother is a 40 year vet in the athletic department. I’ve watched this school transform from d2 to what it is today. All possible by hard working people like my mom.
author: @brandongrissett

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: My fiancé and I live like 20 minutes from the school. I went to high school right down the road from it. It’s crazy expensive, but it is such an incredible campus. We go every year at Christmas time to view the lights. We love it!
author: @kyleniner1155

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: I’m a student here and have worked for the school, it’s not a cult. It’s a good school with a beautiful campus but it has its problems like every school. Yes some rich people go here but there are people like me who are surviving off scholarships and loans and working hard for their degree and a good GPA. Don’t even have a meal plan so the restaurants really are a luxury. Nido is cool and a hard worker, he also does a lot to motivate his students and is a businessman true and true. He works his talent into the school and made it into what it is. Not a cult, just successful and accommodating to everyone rich or poor. (Although some have left due to prices which is understandable)
author: @Princess_Blue12

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: I live in Greensboro, NC which is roughly 30 minutes from the high point campus. Back in 2023 I was offered to play soccer, when I visited the school I was sent a black Escalade to my house since I didn’t live far away and They also let us eat at the steakhouse mentioned in the video, the food was great. I didn’t end up committing to high point I committed to Wake forest to play soccer but now I wish I did bc the campus looks so beautiful.
author: @bkdulaabdullahi

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: I go here - definitely not a cult and they invest in the students heavily. Very expensive but they do offer lots of scholarship - especially if you get accepted to the Honors Program. The amenities are unreal and I’ve loved my time there, gonna be sad to graduate in a year and a half
author: @drewtrey1921

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: Love the video I go here. You showed a lot of what the school has to offer but you gotta get the perspective of a regular student not one with a full ride.
author: @dominicdesiderioscioli2201

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: The statue of the man sitting on the bench with a saxophone is the great jazz artist and saxophone player John Coltrane. He was one of the great pioneers of jazz. He was born near Highpoint.
author: @jodiartman2395

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: As a HP native HPU has many pros and cons. The main issue that our community has with it is that it is absolutely a party college, if you live here and interact with these students daily you'd see the things we see. However it does bring in job opportunities for people but I have heard horror stories of how bad it can be especially for over night security guards dealing with countless reports of sexual violence to the point of hospitalization. My main grievance is when HPU bought out entire neighborhoods for student housing that were predominantly for renters putting so many people at risk, and their still actively trying to buy more neighborhoods mainly the ones surrounding campus. These neighborhoods can be seen as "unsafe" but normal people live here people I know and love and care for that are dealing with the threat of losing the home they've known their entire life. :/
author: @xxpunkbunnyxx

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: It’s crazy seeing videos like this because it looks so pretty. When you’ve worked there 10 years, it just looks like work 😅
author: @BFais-i6m

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: I graduated in 2001 and unfortunately missed out on all of the expansion and improvements made under Nido.  I admit I’m jealous and am amazed every time I step on campus now.  Nido’s vision and investment in the level of education, the experience of the students and preparing them for the future is truly awesome.  We didn’t have anything near a steakhouse when I was there, but we did have a Krispy Kreme and could get a sandwich on any bread we wanted for lunch……oh, and unlimited chicken nuggets……
author: @TommyRich79

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: My daughter started at HPU this year.  We were planning on Baylor which she was also accepted to till we did the tour at HPU
author: @domingoferrari5889

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: This is hilarious! My dad works for HPU and my brother and sister-in-law graduated from there. It’s a fabulous private university. Nothing crazy.
author: @StrawberryFields4ever65

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: My opinion, you give a student everything after awhile what is there to do? Study, 25 per class, that's ideal! And the student said how they teach them manners, the way to conduct themselves, example in dining. They probably teach them how to budget and financial goals. This college is preparing them to be an independent, responsible person. I would love to be young again and go there! More colleges should be like this. I bet the crime rate on campus is.very low. It's based on some companies in Japan. You give your employees all kinds of perks, pay, housing, food, vacations, education to for children and you have the best employee work ethic Kudos to HPU!!!!!
author: @mchyde9645

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: As someone from the area, it seems like most of the controversy with HPU is not with the school itself. It’s more about how the university treats HP and its businesses. That’s where much of the “cult” talk I’ve heard centers around. It also has a reputation as the school you go to if you’re rich, but not smart enough to get into a renowned or esteemed school.
author: @HannahRoyal

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: My husband used to be their fountain supervisor over the fountain techs... their preident is obsessed with fountains. They are all remotely controlled and state of the art. Nearly every residence hall has their own dedicated heated swimming pool as well. He used to tell me about all the extremely expensive cars in the student lots, so there's tons of money flowing in that place. They have kiosks on the corners of about every walkway intersection that hands out breakfast in the mornings to students. They also have a HUGE Christmas display that the community can drive through for about a month.  Qubein himself is loaded, but he does do positive things for the community, including sponsoring the local Children's Museum. My husband doesn't work there anymore, he got a better offer and went elsewhere but he enjoyed that job alot and liked all the people there.
author: @scc52087

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: My mom graduated from High Point College in 1964 and I used to go to some of her classes with her.  I still recognize some of the older buildings from when she went there.  I've attended a couple of their Veteran's Day services, and they really did it up right and treated us well.  They have also planted magnolia trees at a few places around the campus.  There's one that I dedicated to my mom, and she dedicated one to me and one to my sister.  My sister and I were mascots for the cheerleaders back in '62 and '63.
author: @hepcat-bob

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: I'm a native North Carolinian who teaches at Northwestern. I graduated from UNC-Chapel Hill decades ago. The Dean Dome opened during my freshman year, a huge upgrade from Carmichael Auditorium. Neither Northwestern nor UNC has the amenities that HPU has. I remember when HPU was High Point College. I went there to perform at an invitational concert with my choir from UNC. The transformation is AMAZING!
author: @kentbrooks3227

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: they r just protective of their students. i worked construction on the site, and a worker tried to speak to a female on the campus. she reported to someone, and the university ran background checks on all the workers on campus. they escorted every person that was on the sex offenders list that were working there. i say good job. 🤷🏾‍♂️
author: @marcellusharrison9145

title: Inside America’s Most Expensive College Campus (High Point University)
body: High Point University is not the most expensive university in the USA, as matter of fact it is not even the most expensive university in the state of North Carolina.
author: @EyeOfTheWatcher
